meta.title: Tritons’ Fountain
meta.description: Complete travel guide to Tritons’ Fountain in Malta. Learn about this iconic fountain in Floriana, including location and visitor information.

intro.title: Discover Tritons’ Fountain
intro.description: Tritons’ Fountain is one of Malta's most famous landmarks, located in the square outside the City Gate of Valletta, in Floriana. This magnificent bronze fountain, built in 1959 and designed by sculptor Vincent Apap, features three mythological Tritons holding up a massive basin, symbolizing Malta's deep connection with the sea.

visitGuide.title: Visit Tips
items: The fountain square is spacious and flat, perfect for a leisurely walk and taking photos.
The fountain is beautifully illuminated at night, making it an excellent spot for night views and romantic strolls.
The square is highly accessible and serves as the main gateway to the historic city of Valletta.

sections.title: Historical Background
sections.content: Tritons’ Fountain was constructed in 1959, designed by renowned Maltese sculptor Vincent Apap and draughtsman Victor Anastasi. It was built to decorate the newly constructed bus terminus and quickly became a symbol of Malta's independence and national identity.

sections.title: Design & Architecture
sections.content: The main structure of the fountain consists of three bronze Triton figures jointly holding up a massive basin. Water overflows from the basin, creating beautiful cascades. The base is constructed from limestone, echoing the historical architectural style of the surroundings.

sections.title: Cultural Significance
sections.content: As a landmark outside Valletta's City Gate, Tritons’ Fountain is not only a popular photo spot for tourists but also a central square for local residents to gather, celebrate festivals, and host major public events.

sections.title: Restoration & Preservation
sections.content: The fountain underwent a comprehensive restoration between 2017 and 2018, removing years of grime and damage to restore the bronze statues to their original glory. The square was also redesigned to become a fully pedestrianized public space.

hours.title: Visiting Hours
hours.bestTimeSpring: Spring: Pleasant weather, perfect for a stroll in the square
hours.bestTimeSummer: Summer: Enjoy the cool breeze and beautiful night illumination
hours.bestTimeAutumn: Autumn: Mild weather, ideal for taking memorable photos
hours.bestTimeWinter: Winter: Experience the peaceful urban atmosphere
hours.tip: The fountain is located in an open public square and can be visited free of charge at any time.

tickets.title: Admission Information
tickets.parkPrice: As a public landmark, Tritons’ Fountain is completely free and open to all visitors.
tickets.parkingPrice: Paid public parking is available outside the walls of Valletta.
tickets.guidedPrice: No official guided tours are provided, but many Valletta walking tours start here.

transport.title: How to Get Here
transport.fromCenterDesc: Just a 1-minute walk outside the Valletta City Gate to reach the fountain square.
transport.fromStationDesc: Valletta's main bus terminus is adjacent to the fountain, where almost all buses to the capital stop.
transport.fromAirportDesc: Direct airport buses are available to the Valletta bus terminus.
transport.publicTransportDesc: Bus routes from all over Malta have services to the Valletta terminus.
transport.walkingDesc: The square is wide and flat, making it very suitable for walking to the City Gate.
transport.drivingDesc: It is recommended to park at the MCP Car Park and walk.

route.title: Recommended Route
route.overview: Explore Tritons’ Fountain and the surrounding historical area.
steps: Arrive at the Valletta bus terminus; Tritons’ Fountain will be the first sight to greet you
Walk around the fountain to admire the details of the bronze Triton statues
Take a photo with the magnificent Valletta City Gate in the background
Cross the bridge through the City Gate and officially enter the historic old town of Valletta
Upon returning at night, enjoy the fountain's captivating illumination

spots.title: Fountain Front Panorama
spots.description: Stand directly in front of the square to capture the spectacular view of the three Tritons holding the basin.

spots.title: Valletta City Gate Background
spots.description: Use the modern Valletta City Gate as a backdrop to capture the blend of the ancient fountain and modern architecture.

spots.title: Night Illumination
spots.description: When the lights turn on at night, the fountain glows golden, making it an excellent time for night photography.

spots.title: Water Cascades Close-up
spots.description: Get close to capture the dynamic beauty of water cascading from the massive basin.

hotels.name: Valletta Old Town
hotels.desc: Staying within the city walls offers an immersive historical experience, just a few minutes' walk from the fountain.

hotels.name: Floriana
hotels.desc: The district where the fountain is located offers many elegant hotels close to major attractions.

hotels.name: Sliema
hotels.desc: If you prefer a seaside resort vibe, Sliema is a great choice with convenient ferry access to Valletta.

reviews.title: Visitor Reviews
reviews.declaration: Reviews can be viewed via Google Maps.
reviews.moreReviews: View more reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Sarah W.
items.text: A perfect landmark before entering Valletta. The fountain looks absolutely magnificent, especially when lit up at night.

items.name: Marco P.
items.text: A huge square and very clean. You see this spectacular fountain right after getting off the bus, leaving a deep impression.

items.name: Elena R.
items.text: One of Malta's iconic structures. Sitting by the fountain, resting and watching people go by feels very relaxing.

items.name: David T.
items.text: After the renovation, the whole square became a pedestrian zone, free from the previous crowds and exhaust fumes. Perfect for taking photos.

items.name: Anna K.
items.text: The three Triton statues are lifelike, and the combination of bronze and water shows Malta's close connection to the sea.

items.name: James M.
items.text: Although just a fountain, it is the starting point of every trip to the capital Valletta and carries the memories of this city.

officialManagement.title: About Tritons’ Fountain
officialManagement.text: Tritons’ Fountain is a significant landmark and public monument in Malta. The structure is routinely maintained and managed by relevant municipal departments and heritage authorities to ensure its pristine condition as a national symbol.
